---
description: Create or modify a CoreEx Infrastructure-layer repository — EfDb CRUD delegates, QueryArgsConfig queries, BiDirectionMapper, and Result<T> pipelines
---

Guide this workspace through creating or modifying a CoreEx Infrastructure-layer repository.

Use `.github/skills/coreex-repository/SKILL.md` and its referenced workflow as the authoritative workflow when they exist.

Operational contract:
- Ask upfront: entity name, domain (Products=PostgreSQL / Shopping=SQL Server), new or existing, operations, DDD domain layer present.
- Use EfDb delegate shortcuts (GetAsync/CreateAsync/UpdateAsync/DeleteAsync) — never write raw DbContext CRUD.
- DataResult<T> return for Create/Update; DataResult for Delete — includes mutation flag for event decisions.
- Use *WithResultAsync variants + Result<T> pipelines for DDD/Domain-layer repositories.
- BiDirectionMapper: two OnMap overloads (same name, different source type); map Id explicitly; never map ETag or ChangeLog.
- [ScopedService<IInterface>] on every repository class — no manual DI wiring.
- QueryArgsConfig as private static readonly; always .Parse(query).ThrowOnError() before use.
- Always .ConfigureAwait(false) on every await.
- Never create or edit *.g.cs files — those are generated by *.Database / *.CodeGen tooling.
- If any prompt text conflicts with the skill, the skill wins.

Outcome:
- The repository is declared correctly, wired via [ScopedService], builds cleanly, and integration test coverage is offered.
